Title: Leveraging orthology within maize and Arabidopsis QTL to identify genes affecting natural variation in gravitropism
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Arabidopsis Col-0, Ws; maize B73-derived mutant line
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in Arabidopsis columella, meristem, and elongation zone cells; significant ATG5 mRNA in 3-d-old maize primary root; protein evident in germinating kernel
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Primary root tissues during seedling stage
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Arabidopsis loss-of-function mutants (atg5-1, atg5-3); promoter T-DNA activation-tag allele GK_061G06; qPCR; root tip angle time-course phenotyping; root elongation measurement
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter in GK_061G06 T-DNA activation-tag line
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ATG5 positively affects root gravitropism; loss of function impairs curvature, while overexpression enhances tip angle
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Orthologous ATG5 genes reside in maize Chr5-2 and Arabidopsis 5@61 gravitropism QTL. 2. Two Arabidopsis atg5 loss-of-function alleles showed impaired gravitropism without major elongation defects. 3. A promoter T-DNA activation-tag allele strongly overexpressed ATG5 and showed greater tip angle, supporting a positive role in gravitropism.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant root elongation penalty detected in the main assays; broader autophagy perturbation could affect recycling processes
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Natural allelic variation in ATG5 may be useful for tuning root architecture responses; effects should be deployed cautiously because ATG5 is a core autophagy gene
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Within the same QTL interval as UGP2; authors infer both may contribute to the same QTL. This trait is gravitropism rather than nutrient metabolite accumulation.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Arabidopsis segregating fama population; maize QTL context only
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mostly studied in cotyledons; not expressed in roots or only at very low levels
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low or undetectable in roots; inferred developmental role outside root also discussed
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Segregating Arabidopsis fama mutant analysis with post-assay genotyping; root tip angle time-course phenotyping; root elongation measurement
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: FAMA negatively affects gravitropism by reducing overshoot; homozygous fama mutants show overshoot phenotype
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Orthologous FAMA genes lie within maize Chr8-2 and Arabidopsis 3@17 gravitropism QTL. 2. Arabidopsis fama homozygotes displayed increased overshoot in root tip angle curves. 3. Root elongation rate was not altered, indicating an effect on differential growth control rather than general growth.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited direct application because strong loss of function causes sterility; subtle allelic modulation may still be informative for root angle traits
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although FAMA is a transcription factor, the paper focuses on gravitropism, not nutrient metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Arabidopsis Ws; maize B73-derived mutant line
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Typically described as the rate-limiting step in phosphatidylcholine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts phosphorylcholine and CTP into CDP-choline, a key intermediate in phosphatidylcholine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for phosphatidylcholine production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major structural phospholipid of eukaryotic membranes
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Membrane-associated; associates with the face of lipid bilayers
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly expressed in meristem and root apex; maize root apical 5 mm known to express ZmCCT2 well
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root apex/apical 5 mm during seedling stage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Maize Mu insertion mutant analysis; qPCR of root tips; Arabidopsis cct2-1 mutant assay; root tip angle time-course phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 90° gravistimulation; low temperature reported in cited literature to increase CCT2 expression and phosphatidylcholine in rosette leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CCT2 positively affects gravitropism; reduced expression impairs later phase of root curvature
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Orthologous CCT2 genes lie in maize Chr1-2 and Arabidopsis 4@40.3 gravitropism QTL. 2. A maize promoter-proximal Mu insertion reduced ZmCCT2 mRNA in root tips and impaired late-phase gravitropism. 3. Arabidopsis cct2-1 showed a slight but significant gravitropism defect, supporting CCT2 as a conserved causal gene.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely broad membrane-related effects if strongly perturbed
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be used to modulate root response dynamics through natural regulatory variation, but membrane biogenesis roles warrant caution
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper suggests QTL may derive from promoter variation because coding regions were the same between parental alleles in both species.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Arabidopsis Col-0, Ws; maize QTL context only
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: UDP-glucose formation for carbohydrate and cell wall biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: UDP-sugar precursor supply for cellulose/callose/sucrose/glycosylation pathways
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not rate limiting in some contexts per cited work; physiological importance established
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces UDP-glucose from glucose-1-phosphate and UTP, supplying activated glucose for polysaccharide synthesis and other glycosylation reactions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports polysaccharide extension and glycosylation reactions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in Arabidopsis and maize primary root; in Col-0 one splice isoform expresses well in root apex
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Arabidopsis ugp2-2 and ugp2-3 mutant phenotyping; root tip angle time-course assay; root elongation measurement
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: UGP2 dampens gravitropism; mutant alleles show enhanced gravitropism and faster root elongation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Orthologous UGP2 genes lie within maize Chr5-2 and Arabidopsis 5@61 gravitropism QTL. 2. Arabidopsis ugp2 mutants displayed greater gravitropism and faster root elongation. 3. UGP2 and ATG5 likely both contribute to the same QTL, but with opposite effects on gravitropism.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutants showed faster elongation; broader effects on cell wall/carbohydrate metabolism are possible
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Natural alleles may tune root growth/gravitropic dynamics, but pleiotropic impacts on carbohydrate metabolism and growth should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Relationship between faster elongation and stronger gravitropism remains unresolved according to authors.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Arabidopsis segregating fama population; maize QTL context only
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent negative regulator of root gravitropism overshoot
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mostly in cotyledons/stomatal lineage; not expressed in roots or only very lowly
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Segregating mutant analysis with genotyping after assay; root tip angle profiling; root elongation measurement
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative effect on gravitropism overshoot control; mutant increases overshoot
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAMA was prioritized as an orthologous candidate within Arabidopsis and maize QTL. 2. fama homozygotes displayed enhanced overshoot during gravitropism without altered elongation. 3. This reveals a previously unrecognized role for FAMA in root gravitropism.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Only subtle allelic manipulation would be practical due to severe developmental pleiotropy of strong loss-of-function alleles
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is explicitly a transcription factor, although the study is not about nutrient metabolism.
